---
title: "Projects Overview"
---

{{< var course.number >}} includes two independent research projects that together account for **40%** of your final grade. Each project asks you to conduct a complete regression analysis on a dataset of your choosing, applying the concepts and techniques learned in the course.

::: {.callout-tip}
## {{< fa book >}} Project Guide
The [Project Guide]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} provides step-by-step instructions, worked examples, and R code templates for every stage of both projects. Use it as your primary reference throughout.
:::


## Project 1: Linear Regression (20%) {#project-1}

In Project 1, you will conduct a **linear regression analysis** with a continuous outcome variable. This project covers topics from the first half of the course: simple and multiple regression, categorical predictors, confounding, and interactions.

### Timeline

| Component | Due | Weight | Description |
|:----------|:---:|:------:|:------------|
| [Proposal](project-1-proposal.qmd) | Session 7 | 30% | Research question, dataset, variable definitions |
| [Draft](project-1-draft.qmd) | Session 9 | 30% | Complete analysis with output and preliminary interpretation |
| [Final Report](project-1-final.qmd) | Session 11 | 40% | Polished report with all sections |

### Grading

Project 1 is worth **20%** of your final grade, distributed across the three milestones:

| Component | Weight (of Project 1) |
|:----------|:---------------------:|
| {{< fa file-lines >}} Proposal | 30% |
| {{< fa file-lines >}} Draft | 30% |
| {{< fa file-alt >}} Final Report | 40% |


## Project 2: Logistic Regression (20%) {#project-2}

In Project 2, you will conduct a **logistic regression analysis** with a binary outcome variable. This project covers topics from the second half of the course: logistic regression, odds ratios, model diagnostics, and goodness of fit.

### Timeline

| Component | Due | Weight | Description |
|:----------|:---:|:------:|:------------|
| [Proposal](project-2-proposal.qmd) | Session 12 | 20% | Research question, dataset, variable definitions |
| [Draft](project-2-draft.qmd) | Session 14 | 30% | Complete logistic regression analysis with output, diagnostics, and preliminary interpretation |
| [Final Report](project-2-final.qmd) | Session 15 | 50% | Polished report with all sections |

### Grading

Project 2 is worth **20%** of your final grade, distributed across the three milestones:

| Component | Weight (of Project 2) |
|:----------|:---------------------:|
| {{< fa file-lines >}} Proposal | 20% |
| {{< fa file-lines >}} Draft | 30% |
| {{< fa file-alt >}} Final Report | 50% |


## Choosing Your Topic

You may choose **any publicly available dataset** that interests you. For Project 1, your outcome must be a **continuous variable** suitable for linear regression. For Project 2, your outcome must be a **binary variable** suitable for logistic regression. You may use different datasets for the two projects, or use the same dataset with different outcome variables.

The [Project Guide, Ch. 1]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} includes guidance on selecting a dataset, and the [`regdatasets` package]({{< var books.regdatasets >}}){target="_blank"} provides curated options.

::: {.callout-important}
## Dataset Approval
Your dataset must be approved as part of each project's Proposal. Discuss your choice with the instructor if you are unsure whether it is appropriate.
:::


## Submission

All milestones are submitted through **Blackboard**. Each submission should include:

- A rendered PDF or HTML document
- Your R script or `.qmd` source file
- The dataset (or a link to it, if publicly hosted)


## Getting Help

- {{< fa book >}} Start with the [Project Guide]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} for detailed instructions and worked examples
- {{< fa comments >}} Post questions to the Blackboard discussion board
- {{< fa envelope >}} Contact your instructor — see the [Syllabus](/syllabus.qmd) for this term's contact details and office hours
